conbody.p: Impala workload management enables tracking running/completed queries via any sql client.

As queries move through their lifecycle, their state and relevant metrics are tracked by the coordinator responsible for overseeing the query. A dedicated in-memory data structure is used by workload management to manage this data.

Periodically (according to the defined write interval or max queued settings), the workload management processing loop wakes up to process all the queries within this data structure. The data are batched up into a single insert into DML. This DML is executed resulting in the query lifecycle/metrics data to be inserted into an Apache Iceberg table that is backed by a physical disk store.
Since records are inserted into an Apache Iceberg table via a DML statement, an active Impala executor must be part of the cluster.

If an error happens while the DML is running, the data is put back into the in-memory data structure to be processed again the next time the workload managment processing loop wakes up. Inserts for for each query are attemped three times before they are dropped.

Workload management is configured by startup flags set on all coordinator processes. The only required flag is --enable_workload_management=true, but other flags provide flexibilty to tailor workload management to each specified need. The following table lists the common flags that may need to be modified.

row.entry: Boolean. Indicates if workload management is enabled.
String. Indentifies the current cluster. Value will be written into the workload management database table's cluster_id column.
Integer. Number of seconds to wait before inserting completed queries into the query history table. Allows for batching inserts to help avoid small files.

Integer. Maximum number of completed queries that can be queued before they are written to the query history table. Operates independently of the 'query_log_write_interval_m' flag. If the number of queued records reaches this value, the records will be written to the query log table no matter how much time has passed since the last write. A value of 0 indicates no maximum number of queued records.

Integer. Maximum length of a SQL statement that will be recorded in the completed queries table. If a SQL statement with a length longer than this specified value is executed, the SQL inserted into the completed queries table will be trimmed to this length. Any characters that require escaping will have their backslash character counted towards this limit.

Integer. Maximum length of the SQL query plan that will be recorded in the completed queries table. If a query plan has a length longer than this value, the plan inserted into the completed queries table will be trimmed to this length. Any characters that require escaping will have their backslash character counted towards this limit.
String. Specifies a pool or queue used by the queries that insert into the query log table. Empty value causes no pool to be set.
Integer. Specifies the value of the EXEC_TIME_LIMIT_S query option on the DML that inserts records into the sys.impala_query_log table.

conbody.p: Once workload management is enabled, two new tables will be available. Both tables have the exact same structure.

The first table, sys.impala_query_live, is not a typical table since it is not backed by any files on disk. Instead, this table is backed by an in-memory data structure in each Impala coordinator. This table contains all actively running queries and also recently completed queries that have not yet been moved to permanent storage. Data from all coordinators are contained in this table. In other words, this table is not limited to only the queries that are overseen by the coordinator from where this table is being queried.

The second table, sys.impala_query_log, is a regular Apache Iceberg table stored on disk. The workload management processing loop moves records from the in-memory data structures on each coordinator to this table by running insert DMLs. Each coordinator will run its own DML to move data for the queries it has overseen to this table.
Querying either of these tables is no different than querying any other tables. Simply use any SQL client.
Both tables have the following columns.

row.entry: String. Contains the value of the cluster_id coordinator startup flag.
String. Version of the HiveServer (HS2) protocol that was used by the client when connecting.
String. Effective user on the cluster.
String. Username from an authenticated client.
String. Name of the database being queried.
String. Name of the coordinator for the query.
String. Status of the query when it completes.
String. Final state of the query.
String. Final Impala state of the query.
String. Client IP address and port.
Timestamp. Time when the query started. Time zone is in UTC.
Decimal(18,3). Difference between the query end time and start time, in milliseconds (digits after the decimal point represent milliseconds).
String. List of query options stored as a single string containing comma-separated values of key-value pairs.
TIMEZONE=America/Los_Angeles,CLIENT_IDENTIFIER=Impala Shell v4.4.0a1 (04bdb4d) built on Thu Mar 27 10:49:35 PST 2025
String. Name of the resource pool for the query.
Bigint. Size, in bytes of the per-host memory estimate.
Bigint. Size, in bytes of the dedicated coordinator memory estimate.
String. Comma-separated string listing each host and its fragment instances.
Integer. Count of the number of backends used by this query.
String. Result of the admission (not applicable to DDLs).
Integer. Cluster memory, in bytes that was admitted.
String. Name of the executor group.
String. List of all executor groups including the groups that were considered and rejected as part of Workload Aware Auto Scaling.
String. Full text of the executor summary.
Bigint. Number of rows fetched by the query.
Bigint. Count of the number of rows materialized per second.
Decimal(18,3). Time spent materializing rows converted to milliseconds.
Bigint. Count of bytes that were written (or spilled) to scratch disk space.
Decimal(18,3). Event from the timeline. The value represents the number of milliseconds since the query was received.
Decimal(18,3). Event from the timeline. The value represents the number of milliseconds since the query was received.
Decimal(18,3). Event from the timeline. The value represents the number of milliseconds since the query was received.
Decimal(18,3). Event from the timeline. The value represents the number of milliseconds since the query was received.
Decimal(18,3). Event from the timeline. The value represents the number of milliseconds since the query was received.
Decimal(18,3). Event from the timeline. The value represents the number of milliseconds since the query was received.
Decimal(18,3). Event from the timeline. The value represents the number of milliseconds since the query was received.
Decimal(18,3). Event from the timeline. The value represents the number of milliseconds since the query was received.
Bigint. Total read I/O wait time converted to milliseconds.
Bigint. Average read I/O wait time across executors converted to milliseconds.
Bigint. Total bytes read from the data cache.
Bigint. Minimum value of all the per-node peak memory usages.
Bigint. Maximum value of all the per-node peak memory usages.
Bigint. Mean value of all the per-node peak memory usages.
String. SQL statement as provided by the user with Impala's redaction rules applied.
SELECT a.col1, a.col2 from db.tbl a
String. Full text of the query plan.
String. Comma-separated string containing all the tables queried in the SQL statement. Aliased tables are resolved to their actual table names.

String. Comma-separated string containing all columns from the select list of the sql. Aliased columns are resolved to their actual column names. Each column is in the format database.table.column_name.

String. Comma-separated string containing all columns from the where list of the sql. Aliased columns are resolved to their actual column names. Each column is in the format database.table.column_name.
String. Comma-separated string containing all columns from the sql used in a join. Aliased columns are resolved to their actual column names. Each column is in the format database.table.column_name.

String. Comma-separated string containing all columns from the group by and having lists of the sql. Aliased columns are resolved to their actual column names. Each column is in the format database.table.column_name.

String. Comma-separated string containing all columns from the order by list of the sql. Aliased columns are resolved to their actual column names. Each column is in the format database.table.column_name.
Bigint. Number of query slots used by the query on the coordinator.
Bigint. Number of query slots used by the query on the executors. The value in this column represents the slots used by a single executor, not the total number of slots across all executors.

conbody.p: The following metrics are available to help understand what the workload management processing loop is doing.

ul.li: impala-server.completed-queries.failure - The number of completed queries that failed to be written to the query log table. If the same query fails to be written multiple times, each attempt will increase this counter by 1.
impala-server.completed-queries.max-records-writes - Count of the times that completed queries were written to the query log table because the max records queued count was reached.
impala-server.completed-queries.queued - The number of completed queries queued up and waiting to be written to the query log table.
impala-server.completed-queries.scheduled-writes - Count of the times that completed queries were written to the query log table at the regularly scheduled time.
impala-server.completed-queries.write-durations - Histogram. Time spent writing completed queries to the query log table.
impala-server.completed-queries.written - The number of completed queries successfully written to the query log table.

conbody.p: The following log entries are written to help understand what the workload management processing loop is doing.

row.entry: Indicates the workload management setup process that creates/upgrades the sys.impala_query_live and sys.impala_query_log tables has started.
Indicates the workload management setup process has completed successfully.

The workload management processing loop has started its shutdown. Before completing shutdown, an attempt will be made to insert all in-memory queued completed queries into the sys.impala_query_log table.
The workload management processing loop has finished its shutdown processing.

The workload management processing timed out before it successfully completed shutdown processing. The number of completed queries that were not inserted into sys.impala_query_log is included in the log message. These queries that were not inserted are lost.

entry.codeph: Workload management shutdown timed out. Up to '5' queries may have been lost

row.entry: If there is an error running the DML that inserts records from the in-memory queue to the sys.impala_query_log Iceberg table. The included query_id is the id of the insert DML that failed to run.

entry.codeph: could not write completed query table="sys.impala_query_log" query_id="69477a18fdaadfd7:068f25bb00000000"

row.entry: Indicates the DML that inserts records from the in-memory queue to the sys.impala_query_log Iceberg table is too long for Impala to execute.

entry.codeph: Completed queries table insert sql statement of length '9999999999' was longer than the maximum of '2147483647', skipping

row.entry: Logs data about a successful execution of the workload management insert DML.

entry.codeph: wrote completed queries table="sys.impala_query_log" record_count=1 bytes="1.11 KB" gather_time="258.382us" exec_time="1s163ms" query_id="9e4a49a89079233f:c72589eb00000000"

row.entry: Logs data about a failed execution of the workload management insert DML. The error message will also be included in the logs on the next line.

entry.codeph: failed to write completed queries table="sys.impala_query_log" record_count=1 bytes="1.11 KB" gather_time="258.382us" exec_time="1s163ms" query_id="9e4a49a89079233f:c72589eb00000000"

ul.li: sys.impala_query_live - No maintenance is required because this table resides entirely in memory.

sys.impala_query_log -- As an Iceberg table, requires periodic maintenance such as computing statistics and performing snapshot expiration/cleanup. Since each workload is unique, no automatic maintenance is performed on this table and must be manually run.
